← all tutorials

Gossip (Epidemic) Protocols — interactive 📖 GOSSIP_PROTOCOL.md 📄 gossip_protocol.py

📖 Full guide (.md, green) — the long-form written explanation.  📄 Source code (.py, orange) — the ground-truth logic this page is verified against.
One node knows a rumor; each round every node tells one random peer. Like an epidemic, it reaches all N nodes in O(log N) rounds. Switch between push, pull, and push-pull and watch the spread — push has a long tail, push-pull converges in ⌈log₂N⌉+1 rounds. All numbers recompute live from the identical seeded simulation as gossip_protocol.py.

① The spread — push / pull / push-pull on 64 nodes

What to do: pick a mode, drag fanout, then Play or scrub the round slider. Node 0 (top-left) starts infected (green). Newly-infected nodes flash orange; the rest of the infected are gold. Push stalls on the last few; push-pull sweeps the board fast.
node 0 (source) infected newly infected this round uninformed

② Infection curve — simulation vs the epidemic model

What this shows: infected fraction over rounds. Solid line = the seeded simulation (same as panel ①). White line = the mean-field recurrence (push: 1−(1−f)e^−f; pull: 1−(1−f)²; push-pull: 1−(1−f)²e^−f) — it tracks the sim tightly. Dashed line = the textbook SI closed form 1−e^(−2^k/N) (conservative: assumes pure doubling, so it lags push-pull's ~3× growth).
simulation (this seed) mean-field recurrence SI closed form 1−e^(−2^k/N)

③ Three modes compared + the bandwidth cost

Left: convergence rounds for each mode at the current fanout/seed (same N=64). Push-pull always wins. Right: the gossip bandwidth bill — N·F·msg·2/T — moves with the fanout slider. Cassandra runs F=3, T=1s.
check: … JS recompute matches gossip_protocol.py: push-pull (N=64, fanout=1, seed=42) converges in 6 ≤ ⌈log₂64⌉+1=7 rounds, history 1 4 11 28 45 63 64.
